rooms.name: Path
rooms.description: A long dusty path stretches away from the green world behind you. The ground is cracked and dry, and nothing grows except pale weeds that crumble when the wind touches them. Heat waves rise even though the air is not hot. The land feels tired of being land.
rooms.dev_note: // I want this part to feel like lord of the rings

rooms.name: Empty Village
rooms.description: There used to be people here. Houses hang open, market stalls stand bare, and the dry well in the center has been roped off for years. A wraith drifts through the street, but the old hermit sits calmly beside it, listening to the wind as if it is speaking.
rooms.dev_note: // I based this on a ghost town I saw in a documentary

rooms.name: Desert
rooms.description: Sand stretches in every direction beneath an orange sky that does not look mixed correctly. Something moves under the dunes like a stone whale beneath an ocean. Now and then, a buried gleam flashes through the sand before the wind erases your footprints.
rooms.dev_note: // the sand golems are really tough be careful

rooms.name: Wastelands
rooms.description: Beyond the desert, the ground turns black and cracked like cooled fire. The sky hangs low and grey, pressing the horizon flat. In the distance, the ruins of something once magnificent rise from the wasteland like a warning you are walking toward anyway.
rooms.dev_note: // nobody is going to find this area lol

rooms.name: Ruins
rooms.description: The ruins of an ancient civilization tower above you. Broken columns and crumbling walls show people raising a city, forging a crown, and carving a path beneath their own streets. In the center, a pedestal glows. The Ancient Map seems heavier here, as if it knows the floor is hiding a direction.
rooms.description_cleared: The Ruins Guardian is gone. The columns still tower over you, but the carved path beneath the city is easier to see now. For a moment you can almost hear the people who used to live here. Then the wind takes them away.
rooms.dev_note: // this is the most epic area I think. the ruins guardian is the boss

rooms.name: Abandoned Mine
rooms.description: Dark tunnels stretch between rusted minecart tracks and collapsed support beams. The miners dug too deep and found something they should have left buried. Broken tools litter the ground, and the walls are scratched with tally marks where someone counted days by torchlight.
rooms.description_cleared: With the crawler dead, the abandoned mine is quiet enough to hear dust falling from the beams. The tally marks remain, pointing deeper toward the temple the miners uncovered.
rooms.dev_note: // the mine is spooky. I want players to feel like they're going somewhere they shouldn't

rooms.name: Collapsed Temple
rooms.description: The miners broke through into this place by accident. Carved stone walls show a civilization building a crown, a throne, and a kingdom. Then the carvings change: darkness spreading, people fleeing, the crown consuming its wearer. Broken stairs descend beneath the rubble.
rooms.description_cleared: The temple sentinel is still. With the scrape of stone gone, the carved warning is impossible to ignore: the crown was not lost here. It was made here.
rooms.dev_note: // the carvings tell the story of how the corruption started

rooms.name: Buried Sanctum
rooms.description: At the bottom of the temple stairs, a circular chamber has been carved from living rock. Symbols pulse along the walls like a heartbeat. In the center a stone figure kneels: not a statue, but a memory that refused to die. This is where the crown was born.
rooms.description_cleared: The sanctum is quiet now. The Last Keeper has given you their ward and faded into the stone. The symbols on the walls have gone dark. You stand where the corruption was born and where it was finally understood.
rooms.dev_note: // this is the big reveal. the corruption started HERE
